name: bash
description: Execute a bash command and return stdout + stderr. Use for running programs, git, tests, system commands, installing packages, etc. Environment questions ('What Python version?', 'Is X installed?') are tool-use tasks — e.g. bash(command='python --version'). For file creation use write_file instead; for man pages use man instead. Output cap: 256KB (head+tail preserved, middle truncated). Stderr lines prefixed with [stderr].

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. Defaults to 120. Use higher values for long-running commands like test suites or builds.
